items.title: RPU - A Reasoning Processing Unit.
items.abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference performance is increasingly bottlenecked by the memory wall. While GPUs continue to scale raw compute throughput, they struggle to deliver scalable performance for memory bandwidth bound workloads. This challenge is amplified by emerging reasoning LLM applications, where long output sequences, low arithmetic intensity, and tight latency constraints demand significantly higher memory bandwidth. As a result, system utilization drops and energy per inference rises, highlighting the need for an optimized system architecture for scalable memory bandwidth. To address these challenges we present the Reasoning Processing Unit (RPU), a chiplet-based architecture designed to address the challenges of the modern memory wall. RPU introduces: (1) A Capacity-Optimized High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM-CO) that trades capacity for lower energy and cost; (2) a scalable chiplet architecture featuring a bandwidth-first power and area provisioning design; and (3) a decoupled microarchitecture that separates memory, compute, and communication pipelines to sustain high bandwidth utilization. Simulation results show that RPU performs up to $45.3 \times$ lower latency and $18.6 \times$ higher throughput over an H100 system at ISO-TDP on Llama3-405B.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 推理性能越来越受到内存墙的限制。虽然 GPU 继续扩展原始计算吞吐量，但它们很难为内存带宽限制的工作负载提供可扩展的性能。新兴的推理法学硕士应用程序放大了这一挑战，其中长输出序列、低算术强度和严格的延迟限制要求显着更高的内存带宽。结果，系统利用率下降，每次推理的能耗上升，凸显了对可扩展内存带宽的优化系统架构的需求。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了推理处理单元 (RPU)，这是一种基于小芯片的架构，旨在解决现代内存墙的挑战。 RPU 引入了： (1) 容量优化高带宽内存 (HBM-CO)，以容量换取更低的能耗和成本； (2) 可扩展的小芯片架构，具有带宽优先的功率和面积配置设计； (3) 分离的微架构，将内存、计算和通信管道分开，以维持高带宽利用率。仿真结果表明，在 Llama3-405B 上的 ISO-TDP 下，与 H100 系统相比，RPU 的延迟降低了 45.3 美元，吞吐量提高了 18.6 美元。

items.title: Athena: Synergizing Data Prefetching and Off-Chip Prediction via Online Reinforcement Learning.
items.abstract: Prefetching and off-chip prediction are two techniques proposed to hide long memory access latencies in high-performance processors. In this work, we demonstrate that: (1) prefetching and off-chip prediction often provide complementary performance benefits, yet (2) naively combining these two mechanisms often fails to realize their full performance potential, and (3) existing prefetcher control policies (both heuristic- and learning-based) leave significant room for performance improvement behind. Our goal is to design a holistic framework that can autonomously learn to coordinate an off-chip predictor with multiple prefetchers employed at various cache levels, delivering consistent performance benefits across a wide range of workloads and system configurations. To this end, we propose a new technique called Athena, which models the coordination between prefetchers and off-chip predictor (OCP) as a reinforcement learning (RL) problem. Athena acts as the RL agent that observes multiple system-level features (e.g., prefetcher/OCP accuracy, bandwidth usage) over an epoch of program execution, and uses them as state information to select a coordination action (i.e., enabling the prefetcher and/or OCP, and adjusting prefetcher aggressiveness). At the end of every epoch, Athena receives a numerical reward that measures the change in multiple system-level metrics (e.g., number of cycles taken to execute an epoch). Athena uses this reward to autonomously and continuously learn a policy to coordinate prefetchers with OCP. Athena makes a key observation that using performance improvement as the sole RL reward, as used in prior work, is unreliable, as it confounds the effects of the agent's actions with inherent variations in workload behavior. To address this limitation, Athena introduces a composite reward framework that separates (1) system-level metrics directly influenced by Athena's actions (e.g., last-level cache misses) from (2) metrics primarily driven by workload phase changes (e.g., number of mispredicted branches). This allows Athena to autonomously learn a coordination policy by isolating the true impact of its actions from inherent variations in workload behavior. Our extensive evaluation using a diverse set of 100 memoryintensive workloads shows that Athena consistently outperforms multiple prior state-of-the-art coordination policies across a wide range of system configurations with various combinations of underlying prefetchers at various cache levels, OCPs, and main memory bandwidths, while incurring only modest storage overhead and design complexity. The source code of Athena is freely available at https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/Athena.
items.abstract_zh: 预取和片外预测是为了隐藏高性能处理器中较长的存储器访问延迟而提出的两种技术。在这项工作中，我们证明：（1）预取和片外预测通常提供互补的性能优势，但（2）天真地结合这两种机制通常无法充分发挥其性能潜力，（3）现有的预取器控制策略（启发式和基于学习的）为性能改进留下了巨大的空间。我们的目标是设计一个整体框架，可以自主学习协调片外预测器与不同缓存级别使用的多个预取器，从而在各种工作负载和系统配置中提供一致的性能优势。为此，我们提出了一种名为 Athena 的新技术，它将预取器和片外预测器 (OCP) 之间的协调建模为强化学习 (RL) 问题。 Athena 充当 RL 代理，在程序执行的一个时期内观察多个系统级特征（例如，预取器/OCP 准确性、带宽使用情况），并将它们用作状态信息来选择协调操作（即，启用预取器和/或 OCP，并调整预取器攻击性）。在每个 epoch 结束时，Athena 都会收到一个数字奖励，用于衡量多个系统级指标的变化（例如，执行一个 epoch 所需的周期数）。 Athena 使用此奖励来自主持续学习策略以协调预取器与 OCP。 Athena 做出了一个重要的观察，即使用性能改进作为唯一的 RL 奖励（如之前的工作中所使用的那样）是不可靠的，因为它将代理行为的效果与工作负载行为的固有变化混为一谈。为了解决这一限制，Athena 引入了一个复合奖励框架，该框架将 (1) 直接受 Athena 操作影响的系统级指标（例如，最后一级缓存未命中）与 (2) 主要由工作负载阶段变化驱动的指标（例如，错误预测的分支数量）分开。这使得 Athena 能够通过将其操作的真正影响与工作负载行为的固有变化隔离开来自主学习协调策略。我们使用 100 个内存密集型工作负载进行的广泛评估表明，Athena 在各种系统配置中始终优于多种先前最先进的协调策略，并具有不同缓存级别、OCP 和主内存带宽的底层预取器的各种组合，同时仅产生适度的存储开销和设计复杂性。 Athena 的源代码可在 https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/Athena 免费获取。

items.title: Enterprise Class On-Chip Accelerator Integration.
items.abstract: The IBM Z ® platform and the underlying processor chip designs supporting it are optimized for processing vast amounts of data and transactions, while delivering consistent system performance, throughput, and response latencies with a sustained processor utilization of over 90 % under all workload conditions in a highly virtualized and secured computing environment. The IBM Telum® series of processor chip designs that support the platform introduced the industry to a novel modular scalable heterogeneous processor compute framework with an integrated multi-tier unified cache hierarchy all within one chip. The processor chip design leverages a unique approach to ensure all elements work in unison to continuously deliver performance to the evolving needs of mission critical workloads running on the platform while responding to those changing demands at processor clock speeds. This paper will detail the varying compute, accelerator, and cache units within the IBM Telum II processor chip design, how they adaptively work in unison without generating a cacophony of agents competing for scarce hardware resources, and how this forms the backbone of the scalable multi-processor system that our modern economy is built upon.
items.abstract_zh: IBM Z® 平台和支持它的底层处理器芯片设计针对处理大量数据和事务进行了优化，同时提供一致的系统性能、吞吐量和响应延迟，在高度虚拟化和安全的计算环境中的所有工作负载条件下，处理器利用率持续超过 90%。支持该平台的 IBM Telum® 系列处理器芯片设计向业界引入了一种新颖的模块化可扩展异构处理器计算框架，该框架在一个芯片内集成了多层统一缓存层次结构。处理器芯片设计利用独特的方法来确保所有元素协同工作，持续提供性能以满足平台上运行的关键任务工作负载不断变化的需求，同时以处理器时钟速度响应这些不断变化的需求。本文将详细介绍 IBM Telum II 处理器芯片设计中不同的计算、加速器和缓存单元，它们如何自适应地协同工作，而不产生争夺稀缺硬件资源的代理的杂音，以及它们如何构成我们现代经济所依赖的可扩展多处理器系统的支柱。

items.title: Characterizing Cloud-Native LLM Inference at Bytedance and Exposing Optimization Challenges and Opportunities for Future AI Accelerators.
items.abstract: As a major provider of LLM inference services, ByteDance has continuously explored diverse accelerator options to meet the rapidly growing inference demands of various heterogeneous LLM scenarios with higher cost-effectiveness, thereby enabling LLMs to serve more people worldwide. However, during this process, we have found that the complexity and opacity of cloud scenarios and corresponding cloud accelerators make it difficult for academia and many innovative chip startups to fully understand the real demands and challenges of these scenarios, which in turn severely restricts innovation and application potential in this field. To bridge this gap, we first present and analyze the data and characteristics of the ByteDance Doubao LLM app across multiple dimensions, helping the community understand real-world cloud scenarios, and detail the challenges and opportunities we have identified. Second, we propose and plan to open-source our multi-level evaluation framework, XPU-Perf, which includes benchmarks spanning instructions, operators, and models. This framework improves interpretability and trustworthiness, and helps promising new accelerator architectures gain wider adoption and development. Finally, we present comparative results of four typical accelerators, summarize their shortcomings and challenges, conduct in-depth analysis, and highlight numerous architectural and scheduling innovation opportunities we have observed.
items.abstract_zh: 作为LLM推理服务的主要提供商，字节跳动不断探索多样化的加速器方案，以更高的成本效益满足各种异构LLM场景快速增长的推理需求，从而使LLM能够为全球更多的人服务。但在这个过程中，我们发现云场景和相应的云加速器的复杂性和不透明性，使得学术界和很多创新芯片初创公司很难充分理解这些场景的真实需求和挑战，进而严重制约了该领域的创新和应用潜力。为了弥补这一差距，我们首先从多个维度呈现并分析字节跳动豆宝LLM应用程序的数据和特征，帮助社区了解真实的云场景，并详细说明我们发现的挑战和机遇。其次，我们提议并计划开源我们的多级评估框架 XPU-Perf，其中包括跨越指令、运算符和模型的基准。该框架提高了可解释性和可信度，并有助于有前途的新加速器架构获得更广泛的采用和开发。最后，我们展示了四种典型加速器的比较结果，总结了它们的缺点和挑战，进行了深入分析，并重点介绍了我们观察到的众多架构和调度创新机会。

items.title: CROPHE: Cross-Operator Dataflow Optimization for Fully Homomorphic Encryption Accelerators.
items.abstract: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) enables the protection of data privacy at the cost of significantly higher computational demands. To alleviate its memory-bound bottlenecks, dataflow optimizations that maximize on-chip data reuse and minimize off-chip accesses could be leveraged. In this work, we exploit the opportunities of cross-operator dataflow optimizations in FHE accelerators, and propose a hardware-software co-design called CROPHE. On the hardware level, instead of overly-specialized functional units, CROPHE provisions a homogeneous and unified architecture that allows for flexible resource allocation and operator mapping. On the software level, the scheduling framework of CROPHE takes a comprehensive and systematic approach to explore various spatial and temporal data pipelining and sharing schemes across multiple operators, resulting in more efficient dataflow than prior work. We also propose novel cross-operator dataflow optimizations for the unique operators in FHE including number theoretic transforms and homomorphic rotations. The evaluation shows CROPHE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art designs by $1.77 \times$ to $4.86 \times$ .
items.abstract_zh: 全同态加密 (FHE) 能够保护数据隐私，但代价是显着提高计算需求。为了缓解其内存限制瓶颈，可以利用数据流优化来最大化片上数据重用并最小化片外访问。在这项工作中，我们利用 FHE 加速器中跨运营商数据流优化的机会，并提出了一种称为 CROPHE 的软硬件协同设计。在硬件层面，CROPHE没有提供过于专业的功能单元，而是提供了同构统一的架构，可以实现灵活的资源分配和运营商映射。在软件层面，CROPHE的调度框架采用全面、系统的方法来探索跨多个运营商的各种时空数据管道和共享方案，从而产生比以前的工作更高效的数据流。我们还为 FHE 中的独特算子提出了新颖的跨算子数据流优化，包括数论变换和同态旋转。评估显示 CROPHE 的性能明显优于最先进的设计 1.77 美元到 4.86 美元。

items.title: A Deadlock-Free Bridge Module for Inter-Chiplet Cache-Coherent Communication in an Open Chiplet Ecosystem.
items.abstract: The envisioned open chiplet ecosystem promises significant reductions in chip design complexity and cost by enabling designers to rapidly assemble standard chiplets from diverse vendors. Constructing such an open chiplet ecosystem requires support from Network-on-Chip (NoC) routing algorithms, as integrating multiple chiplets onto an interposer can potentially lead to inter-chiplet deadlock. Prior work avoids deadlock through methods like turn restrictions, virtual channel isolation, or packet injection control, or recovers from deadlock using mechanisms such as escape channels or bubble flow control. These approaches achieve a favorable balance regarding modularity, performance, and cost. However, they still rely on designers possessing detailed knowledge of the internal NoC architecture within each chiplet. This requirement impedes the development of a truly open ecosystem, as it constrains chiplet interoperability and vendor independence. Addressing this limitation, we propose a Deadlock-Free Bridge Module (DFBM) designed to resolve interchiplet deadlock without relying on the specifics of individual chiplet NoC implementations. The DFBM infers the transmission behavior of inter-chiplet packets by analyzing the dependency relationships among coherence protocol transaction flows. It then employs a packet injection control mechanism to isolate inter- and intra-chiplet packets, thereby preventing deadlock. DFBMs can be seamlessly interconnected between arbitrary chiplets to achieve deadlock-freedom, eliminating the need for modifications to their internal NoC architectures. Experimental results demonstrate that DFBM incurs only 2.5% area overhead, while achieving a performance improvement ranging from 1% to 7%.
items.abstract_zh: 设想的开放式小芯片生态系统使设计人员能够快速组装来自不同供应商的标准小芯片，从而有望显着降低芯片设计的复杂性和成本。构建这样一个开放的小芯片生态系统需要片上网络 (NoC) 路由算法的支持，因为将多个小芯片集成到中介层上可能会导致小芯片间的死锁。先前的工作通过轮次限制、虚拟通道隔离或数据包注入控制等方法避免死锁，或使用逃逸通道或气泡流控制等机制从死锁中恢复。这些方法在模块化、性能和成本方面实现了良好的平衡。然而，他们仍然依赖于设计人员掌握每个小芯片内部 NoC 架构的详细知识。这一要求阻碍了真正开放的生态系统的发展，因为它限制了小芯片的互操作性和供应商的独立性。为了解决这一限制，我们提出了一种无死锁桥接模块 (DFBM)，旨在解决芯片间死锁，而不依赖于各个芯片片 NoC 实现的具体细节。 DFBM 通过分析一致性协议事务流之间的依赖关系来推断小芯片间数据包的传输行为。然后，它采用数据包注入控制机制来隔离小芯片间和芯片内数据包，从而防止死锁。 DFBM 可以在任意小芯片之间无缝互连，以实现无死锁，从而无需修改其内部 NoC 架构。实验结果表明，DFBM 仅产生 2.5% 的面积开销，同时实现了 1% 至 7% 的性能提升。

items.title: DSAssassin: Cross-VM Side-Channel Attacks by Exploiting Intel Data Streaming Accelerator.
items.abstract: Modern datacenter infrastructures increasingly integrate bespoke accelerators to offload specific workloads from CPUs. Among them, Intel's Data Streaming Accelerator (DSA), supported by Intel VT-d, is deployed in the latest Xeon processors. However, its security implications remain unexplored. In this paper, we propose DSAssassin, a novel side-channel attack launched from DSA. Although Intel's scalable I/O virtualization mitigates device-based threats, we show that DSA introduces a new attack surface that bypasses such protections. By reverse-engineering the Device TLB (DevTLB) introduced by DSA, we find that it is not isolated by different processes. We further analyze DSA's shared work queue (SWQ) and uncover a timer-free side channel via DMWr transaction. Building on these insights, we develop two attack primitives: a DevTLB-based timing attack and an SWQ-based contention attack. These enable cross-VM covert- and side-channel attacks. First, the cross-VM covert-channel can achieve a true capability of 17.19 Kbps with a 4.64% error rate—5× faster and 4× more accurate than the latest work. Second, we present a website fingerprinting attack on the top 100 websites, and the classification accuracy can reach 85.7%. Finally, we demonstrate a cross-VM keystroke inference attack with $F_{1}$ scores of 92.0% (DevTLB) and 98.4% (SWQ)—outperforming the state-of-the-art.
items.abstract_zh: 现代数据中心基础设施越来越多地集成定制加速器，以卸载 CPU 上的特定工作负载。其中，由英特尔VT-d支持的英特尔数据流加速器（DSA）被部署在最新的至强处理器中。然而，其安全影响仍未得到探索。在本文中，我们提出了 DSAssassin，这是一种从 DSA 发起的新型侧通道攻击。尽管英特尔的可扩展 I/O 虚拟化可以缓解基于设备的威胁，但我们表明 DSA 引入了绕过此类保护的新攻击面。通过对DSA引入的Device TLB（DevTLB）进行逆向工程，我们发现它并没有被不同进程隔离。我们进一步分析 DSA 的共享工作队列 (SWQ)，并通过 DMWr 事务发现无定时器侧通道。基于这些见解，我们开发了两种攻击原语：基于 DevTLB 的定时攻击和基于 SWQ 的争用攻击。这些可以实现跨虚拟机的隐蔽和旁道攻击。首先，跨虚拟机隐蔽通道可以实现 17.19 Kbps 的真实能力，错误率为 4.64%，比最新工作快 5 倍，准确率提高 4 倍。其次，我们对排名前100的网站进行网站指纹攻击，分类准确率可以达到85.7%。最后，我们演示了跨虚拟机击键推理攻击，其 $F_{1}$ 得分为 92.0% (DevTLB) 和 98.4% (SWQ)，优于最先进的水平。

items.title: Conflux: A High-Performance Keyword Private Retrieval System for Dynamic Datasets.
items.abstract: Homomorphic Encryption (HE)-based Private Information Retrieval (PIR) allows clients to retrieve plaintext records from untrusted servers without revealing query content. While promising in theory, existing solutions fall short in practice due to two fundamental limitations: (1) poor support for dynamic datasets, which limits applicability in real-world, evolving workloads; and (2) excessive I/O overhead from full-database scans. These bottlenecks prevent current designs from bridging the gap between cryptographic privacy and system efficiency. In this paper, we introduce Conflux, an efficient keyword PIR system for dynamic data environments through a protocolarchitecture co-design approach. At the protocol level, Conflux employs a novel two-phase retrieval mechanism, consisting of an oblivious filtering phase followed by a precise retrieval phase. This design natively supports efficient online insertions, deletions, and updates, while maintaining near-optimal computational complexity. At the system level, Conflux adopts a heterogeneous accelerator architecture that tightly couples computational storage devices and incorporates software-hardware co-optimization techniques to mitigate I/O bottlenecks. Experimental results show that Conflux reduces query processing time by up to $2.64 \times$ compared to the state-of-the-art methods, while retaining full support for dynamic datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 基于同态加密 (HE) 的私有信息检索 (PIR) 允许客户端从不受信任的服务器检索明文记录，而无需泄露查询内容。虽然理论上很有希望，但现有的解决方案在实践中存在不足，因为有两个基本限制：(1) 对动态数据集的支持较差，这限制了在现实世界、不断变化的工作负载中的适用性； (2) 全数据库扫描产生过多的 I/O 开销。这些瓶颈阻碍了当前设计弥合加密隐私和系统效率之间的差距。在本文中，我们介绍了 Conflux，这是一种通过协议架构协同设计方法用于动态数据环境的高效关键字 PIR 系统。在协议层面，Conflux 采用了一种新颖的两阶段检索机制，包括不经意的过滤阶段和精确检索阶段。这种设计本身支持高效的在线插入、删除和更新，同时保持接近最优的计算复杂性。在系统层面，Conflux采用异构加速器架构，将计算存储设备紧密耦合，并结合软硬件协同优化技术来缓解I/O瓶颈。实验结果表明，与最先进的方法相比，Conflux 将查询处理时间减少了高达 $2.64 \times$，同时保留了对动态数据集的完全支持。

items.title: PASCAL: A Phase-Aware Scheduling Algorithm for Serving Reasoning-based Large Language Models.
items.abstract: The emergence of reasoning-based LLMs leveraging Chain-of-Thought (CoT) inference introduces new serving challenges, as their extended reasoning phases delay user-visible output and inflate Time-To-First-Token (TTFT). Existing LLM serving frameworks fail to distinguish between reasoning and answering phases, leading to performance degradation under GPU memory constraints. We present PASCAL, a phase-aware scheduling algorithm that prioritizes reasoning to reduce TTFT while using controlled preemption and token pacing during answering to preserve Quality-of-Experience (QoE). Our hierarchical scheduler combines instance-level placement with intrainstance execution and enables dynamic migration at phase boundaries to balance load and reduce interference. Across benchmarks using DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B, Pascal reduces tail TTFT by up to 72 % while maintaining answering phase SLO attainment, demonstrating the importance of phaseaware scheduling for reasoning-based LLM deployment.
items.abstract_zh: 利用思想链 (CoT) 推理的基于推理的法学硕士的出现带来了新的服务挑战，因为它们的扩展推理阶段延迟了用户可见的输出并增加了首次令牌时间 (TTFT)。现有的 LLM 服务框架无法区分推理和回答阶段，导致 GPU 内存限制下的性能下降。我们提出了 PASCAL，一种阶段感知调度算法，它优先考虑推理以减少 TTFT，同时在应答期间使用受控抢占和令牌节奏来保持体验质量 (QoE)。我们的分层调度程序将实例级放置与实例内执行相结合，并支持阶段边界的动态迁移，以平衡负载并减少干扰。在使用 DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B 的基准测试中，Pascal 将尾部 TTFT 减少了高达 72%，同时保持了回答阶段 SLO 的成绩，这证明了阶段感知调度对于基于推理的 LLM 部署的重要性。

items.title: zkPHIRE: A Programmable Accelerator for ZKPs over HIgh-degRee, Expressive Gates.
items.abstract: Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) have emerged as a powerful tool for secure and privacy-preserving computation. ZKPs enable one party to convince another of a statement's validity without revealing anything else. This capability has profound implications in many domains, including: machine learning, blockchain, image authentication, and electronic voting. Despite their potential, ZKPs have seen limited deployment because of their exceptionally high computational overhead, which manifests primarily during proof generation. To mitigate these overheads, a (growing) body of researchers have proposed hardware accelerators and GPU implementations of kernels and complete protocols. Prior art spans a wide variety of ZKP schemes that vary significantly in computational overhead, proof size, verifier cost, protocol setup, and trust. The latest, and widely used ZKP protocols are intentionally designed to balance these trade-offs. A particular challenge in modern ZKP systems is supporting complex, high-degree gates using the SumCheck protocol. We address this challenge with a novel programmable accelerator to efficiently handle arbitrary custom gates via SumCheck. Our accelerator achieves upwards of $1000 \times$ geomean speedup over CPU-based SumChecks across a range of gate types. We include this unit in zkPHIRE, a programmable, full-system accelerator that accelerates the HyperPlonk protocol. zkPHIRE achieves $1486 \times$ geomean speedup over CPU and $11.87 \times$ geomean speedup over the state-of-the-art at iso-area. Together, these results demonstrate compelling performance while scaling to large problem sizes (upwards of 2 30 constraints) and maintaining small proof sizes ( $4-5$ KB).
items.abstract_zh: 零知识证明（ZKP）已成为安全和隐私保护计算的强大工具。 ZKP 使一方能够在不透露任何其他信息的情况下让另一方相信某项声明的有效性。这种能力在许多领域具有深远的影响，包括：机器学习、区块链、图像认证和电子投票。尽管 ZKP 具有潜力，但由于其极高的计算开销（主要体现在证明生成过程中），其部署仍受到限制。为了减轻这些开销，越来越多的研究人员提出了硬件加速器以及内核和完整协议的 GPU 实现。现有技术涵盖了各种各样的 ZKP 方案，这些方案在计算开销、证明大小、验证者成本、协议设置和信任方面差异很大。最新且广泛使用的 ZKP 协议旨在平衡这些权衡。现代 ZKP 系统中的一个特殊挑战是使用 SumCheck 协议支持复杂的高级门。我们使用新颖的可编程加速器来应对这一挑战，通过 SumCheck 有效处理任意自定义门。我们的加速器在一系列门类型上比基于 CPU 的 SumCheck 实现了高达 1000 美元\times$几何平均加速。我们将此单元包含在 zkPHIRE 中，这是一个可编程的全系统加速器，可加速 HyperPlonk 协议。 zkPHIRE 比 CPU 实现了 $1486 \times$ geomean 加速，比 iso 区域最先进的 $11.87 \times$ geomean 加速。总之，这些结果展示了令人信服的性能，同时扩展到较大的问题规模（最多 2 30 个约束）并保持较小的证明大小（$4-5$ KB）。

items.title: Pulse: Fine-Grained Hierarchical Hashing Index for Disaggregated Memory.
items.abstract: By decoupling compute and memory resources into independent pools that are provisioned and managed separately, disaggregated memory (DM) is promising to break the scaling constraints for memory systems and improve resource utilization. However, it also comes with a new challenge to design a high-performance hashing index to manage the vast memory pool with weak computing power. In this paper, we reconsider this problem and find that existing hashing indexes for DM still experience two fundamental yet unresolved limitations: (i) amplifying traffic under high insertion concurrency, and (ii) introducing significantly high insertion latency, stemming mainly from the directory synchronization and item relocation in resizing process. We resolve the above limitations by designing Pulse, a finegrained hierarchical hashing index for DM. Pulse comprises the following three design primitives. It proposes a multi-level index structure, which breaks the conventional flat directory into multiple sub-directories that are organized hierarchically, achieving fine-grained directory synchronization. Pulse also maintains a small portion of hashed keys in the memory pool, which aids in item relocation during resizing, thereby reducing resizing traffic and promising system stability. Pulse finally exploits operation parallelism by tailoring the doorbell batching mechanism with selective signaling. We conduct extensive experiments using a variety of benchmarks, showing that Pulse can improve $3.46 \times$ of the throughput and reduce 76.3% of the tail latency compared to state-of-the-art hashing indexes.
items.abstract_zh: 通过将计算和内存资源解耦到单独配置和管理的独立池中，分解内存 (DM) 有望打破内存系统的扩展限制并提高资源利用率。然而，它也带来了新的挑战，即设计高性能的哈希索引来管理计算能力较弱的庞大内存池。在本文中，我们重新考虑这个问题，发现现有的 DM 哈希索引仍然存在两个基本但尚未解决的局限性：（i）在高插入并发下放大流量，以及（ii）引入显着较高的插入延迟，这主要源于调整大小过程中的目录同步和项目重定位。我们通过设计 Pulse（一种用于 DM 的细粒度分层哈希索引）解决了上述限制。 Pulse 包含以下三个设计原语。它提出了一种多级索引结构，将传统的平面目录打破为多个分层组织的子目录，实现细粒度的目录同步。 Pulse 还在内存池中维护一小部分散列键，这有助于在调整大小期间重新定位项目，从而减少调整大小流量并保证系统稳定性。 Pulse 最终通过使用选择性信号定制门铃批处理机制来利用操作并行性。我们使用各种基准进行了广泛的实验，结果表明，与最先进的哈希索引相比，Pulse 可以提高 3.46 美元的吞吐量，并减少 76.3% 的尾部延迟。

items.title: PinDrop: Breaking the Silence on SDCs in a Large-Scale Fleet.
items.abstract: Silent Data Corruptions (SDCs) pose a significant and often hidden threat to the reliability of large-scale computing infrastructure, as they can silently compromise data integrity without immediate detection. Detecting such behaviors at hyperscale is often challenging due to their intermittent nature and the vast diversity of hardware and workloads in a large-scale fleet. This work addresses these challenges by introducing PinDrop, a characterization methodology that leverages continuous, high-frequency testing infrastructure across millions of servers to gather information about SDCs at scale. By leveraging extensive test-suites tailored to mimic real-world applications and exercise a wide range of CPU features, we provide the most comprehensive characterization of SDC failures to date, analyzing over 500 million test executions across millions of devices. Our findings reveal that 0.035% of tested machines suffer from at least one SDC failure during their lifetime. Examining years of data (rather than just a testing snapshot in time), we observe SDCs emerging long after initial deployment and persisting over time. Detailed analysis shows that an average of 0.0024% of tested machines begin failing in each quarter they are tested beyond an initial burn-in period, confirming a fundamental need for continuous testing. Our findings also provide further insights into SDC behaviors at-scale, including failure breakdowns across architectures, test families, specific core IDs, and output-level behaviors.
items.abstract_zh: 静默数据损坏 (SDC) 对大规模计算基础设施的可靠性构成了重大且通常是隐藏的威胁，因为它们可以在不立即检测到的情况下悄悄损害数据完整性。由于其间歇性以及大规模机群中硬件和工作负载的巨大多样性，在超大规模上检测此类行为通常具有挑战性。这项工作通过引入 PinDrop 来解决这些挑战，PinDrop 是一种表征方法，利用跨数百万台服务器的连续、高频测试基础设施来大规模收集有关 SDC 的信息。通过利用为模拟真实应用程序而定制的广泛测试套件并运用各种 CPU 功能，我们提供了迄今为止最全面的 SDC 故障特征分析，分析了数百万台设备上的 5 亿多个测试执行情况。我们的研究结果表明，0.035% 的测试机器在其使用寿命期间至少遭遇过一次 SDC 故障。通过检查多年的数据（而不仅仅是及时的测试快照），我们观察到 SDC 在初始部署后很长时间内出现，并随着时间的推移持续存在。详细分析表明，平均有 0.0024% 的测试机器在超出初始老化期后的每个季度开始出现故障，这证实了持续测试的基本需求。我们的研究结果还提供了对大规模 SDC 行为的进一步见解，包括跨架构、测试系列、特定核心 ID 和输出级行为的故障故障。

items.title: BitDecoding: Unlocking Tensor Cores for Long-Context LLMs with Low-Bit KV Cache.
items.abstract: The rise of long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) amplifies memory and bandwidth demands during autoregressive decoding, as the Key-Value (KV) cache grows with each generated token. Low-bit KV-cache quantization (e.g., 4-bit or 2-bit) can reduce memory footprint while preserving accuracy, but existing systems suffer from slow decoding due to their exclusive reliance on CUDA cores, neglecting Tensor Cores—the primary source of compute on modern GPUs. We present BitDecoding, a new long-context LLMs inference system with low-bit KV cache. BitDecoding enables efficient low-bit KV cache decoding by cooperatively leveraging CUDA Cores and Tensor Cores. It introduces methods for automatically inducing optimized layouts to exploit Tensor Cores, along with novel warp-level parallelization strategies for dequantization. For unified system support, BitDecoding includes a query transformation module supporting diverse attention variants, a quantization kernel to support both tensor-wise and channelwise scaling used in various quantization algorithms with high performance, and a dequantization kernel with a softwaredefined pipeline to coordinate CUDA and Tensor Cores execution for mix-precision operations. In addition, architecture-specific optimizations leverage Hopper's warpgroup tensor instructions and Blackwell's native low-precision tensor formats to maximize decoding throughput on the latest GPU generations. Evaluated on Blackwell, Hopper, Ada, and Ampere architectures, BitDecoding attains on average a 7.5× decoding speedup over FP16 FlashDecoding-v2, and further reaches up to 8.6× with native MXFP4 formats on Blackwell, while surpassing the state-of-the-art low-bit system QServe by up to 4.3×. On LLaMA-3.1-8B with a 128K context, BitDecoding reduces singlebatch decoding latency by 3×, demonstrating substantial improvements for long-context generation, and is open sourced at https://github.com/OpenBitSys/BitDecoding.
items.abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型 (LLM) 的兴起放大了自回归解码期间的内存和带宽需求，因为键值 (KV) 缓存随着每个生成的令牌而增长。低位 KV 缓存量化（例如 4 位或 2 位）可以在保持准确性的同时减少内存占用，但现有系统由于完全依赖 CUDA 核心，而忽略了张量核心（现代 GPU 上的主要计算源），因此解码速度较慢。我们推出了 BitDecoding，一种具有低位 KV 缓存的新型长上下文 LLM 推理系统。 BitDecoding 通过协同利用 CUDA 核心和 Tensor 核心来实现高效的低位 KV 缓存解码。它引入了自动引入优化布局以利用张量核心的方法，以及用于反量化的新颖的扭曲级并行化策略。为了实现统一系统支持，BitDecoding 包括支持不同注意力变体的查询转换模块、支持各种高性能量化算法中使用的张量和通道缩放的量化内核，以及具有软件定义管道的反量化内核，用于协调 CUDA 和张量核心执行以实现混合精度操作。此外，特定于架构的优化利用 Hopper 的 warpgroup 张量指令和 Blackwell 的本机低精度张量格式来最大限度地提高最新一代 GPU 的解码吞吐量。在 Blackwell、Hopper、Ada 和 Ampere 架构上进行评估，BitDecoding 比 FP16 FlashDecoding-v2 平均获得 7.5 倍的解码加速，并且在 Blackwell 上使用本机 MXFP4 格式进一步达到 8.6 倍，同时超过最先进的低位系统 QServe 高达 4.3 倍。在具有 128K 上下文的 LLaMA-3.1-8B 上，BitDecoding 将单批解码延迟减少了 3 倍，展示了长上下文生成的重大改进，并且在 https://github.com/OpenBitSys/BitDecoding 上开源。

items.title: Streamlined on-Chip Temporal Prefetching.
items.abstract: In this paper, we present the Streamline temporal prefetcher, which introduces a stream-based metadata representation that produces three significant benefits over Triangel, the previous state-of-the-art in temporal prefetching. First, it removes redundancy present in Triangel's metadata. Second, it prioritizes the storage of those metadata entries that have higher prefetch utility. Third, it eliminates the need for the untenable LLC traffic that is induced when Triangel dynamically adjusts the size of its metadata store. The end result is that for memory-intensive SPEC 2006, SPEC 2017, and GAP benchmarks, Streamline outperforms Triangel by 6.7 percentage points on an 8-core system with a stride prefetcher. This performance benefit comes from Streamline's improved storage efficiency: It holds 33% more correlations than Triangel, which translates to $\mathbf{1 2. 5}$ percentage points better prefetch coverage. Significantly, Streamline matches the performance of Triangel even when Triangel is given twice the metadata storage.
items.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 Streamline 时间预取器，它引入了基于流的元数据表示，与 Triangel（之前的时间预取领域最先进的技术）相比，它具有三个显着优势。首先，它消除了 Triangel 元数据中存在的冗余。其次，它优先存储那些具有较高预取效用的元数据条目。第三，它消除了 Triangel 动态调整其元数据存储大小时产生的无法维持的 LLC 流量的需要。最终结果是，对于内存密集型 SPEC 2006、SPEC 2017 和 GAP 基准测试，在具有跨步预取器的 8 核系统上，Streamline 的性能比 Triangel 高出 6.7 个百分点。这种性能优势来自于 Streamline 改进的存储效率：它比 Triangel 拥有多 33% 的相关性，这意味着预取覆盖率提高了 $\mathbf{1 2. 5}$ 个百分点。值得注意的是，即使 Triangel 的元数据存储量是两倍，Streamline 的性能也能与 Triangel 相媲美。

items.title: Tempranillo: Non-Speculative Early Register Release.
items.abstract: Limited by the breakdown of technology scaling, CPU architects are looking for creative solutions to deliver performance improvements while unable to traditionally scale up microarchitectural structures. One promising approach is engineering a microarchitecture that uses resources more efficiently, for example, by recycling them faster to relieve pressure on critical structures and making them look bigger than they are. The physical register file (PRF) is a key structure that faces severe area and power constraints that limit its (and the whole CPU's) scalability. Based on this observation, previous work proposed solutions to reduce the pressure on the PRF by reducing the time each register remains allocated. In this paper, we corroborate earlier findings that the early release of registers is a promising approach to reduce the occupancy of the PRF and we identify novel tight conditions for safely releasing registers. Based on this analysis, we design Tempranillo: an aggressive, non-speculative microarchitecture to release registers as early as possible without requiring additional recovery mechanisms. Tempranillo delivers up to 3.3 % and 11.8 % performance improvement over conventional release on singlethreaded and a 2 -way SMT CPUs, respectively. Additionally, Tempranillo requires modest storage overheads, translating into a performance improvement per KiB of storage of up to 2.6 % and 9.3 % for single-thread and 2-way SMT, respectively. Our evaluation shows that Tempranillo improves over both the state-of-the-art non-speculative and speculative proposals.
items.abstract_zh: 受技术扩展崩溃的限制，CPU 架构师正在寻找创造性的解决方案来提高性能，同时无法以传统方式扩展微架构结构。一种有前途的方法是设计一种更有效地利用资源的微架构，例如，通过更快地回收资源来减轻关键结构的压力，并使它们看起来比实际更大。物理寄存器文件 (PRF) 是一个关键结构，面临着严格的面积和功耗限制，限制了其（以及整个 CPU）的可扩展性。基于这一观察，之前的工作提出了通过减少每个寄存器保持分配的时间来减轻 PRF 压力的解决方案。在本文中，我们证实了早期的发现，即早期释放寄存器是减少 PRF 占用的一种有前途的方法，并且我们确定了安全释放寄存器的新的严格条件。基于此分析，我们设计了 Tempranillo：一种积极的、非推测性的微架构，可以尽早释放寄存器，而不需要额外的恢复机制。与传统版本相比，Tempranillo 在单线程和 2 路 SMT CPU 上的性能分别提高了 3.3% 和 11.8%。此外，Tempranillo 需要适度的存储开销，对于单线程和 2 路 SMT，每 KiB 存储的性能分别提高了 2.6% 和 9.3%。我们的评估表明，丹魄比最先进的非投机性和投机性提案都有所改进。

items.title: GenPairX: A Hardware-Algorithm Co-Designed Accelerator for Paired-End Read Mapping.
items.abstract: Genome sequencing has become a central focus in computational biology due to its critical role in applications such as personalized medicine, disease outbreak tracking, and evolutionary research. A genome study typically begins with sequencing, which produces millions to billions of short DNA fragments known as reads. Extracting meaningful biological insights from these reads requires a computationally intensive step called read mapping, where each read is aligned to a reference genome. Read mapping for short reads comes in two forms: single-end and paired-end, with the latter being more prevalent due to its higher accuracy and support for advanced analysis. Read mapping remains a major performance bottleneck in genome analysis as a result of the extensive use of computationally intensive dynamic programming. Prior efforts have attempted to mitigate this cost by employing filters to identify and potentially discard computationally expensive matches and leveraging hardware accelerators to speed up the computations. While partially effective, these approaches have limitations. In particular, existing filters are often ineffective for paired-end reads, as they evaluate each read independently and exhibit relatively low filtering ratios. In this work, we propose GenPairX, a hardware-algorithm codesigned accelerator that efficiently minimizes the computational load of paired-end read mapping while enhancing the throughput of memory-intensive operations. GenPairX introduces: (1) a novel filtering algorithm that jointly considers both reads in a pair to improve filtering effectiveness, and a lightweight alignment algorithm to replace most of the computationally expensive dynamic programming operations, and (2) two specialized hardware mechanisms to support the proposed algorithms. The proposed hardware addresses the high memory bandwidth demands of the read filtering process via orchestration of memory accesses over high-bandwidth memory channels, and accelerates the alignment of candidate reads via simple vectorized logical XOR operators. Our evaluations show that GenPairX delivers substantial performance improvements over state-of-the-art solutions, achieving $1575 \times$ and $1.43 \times$ higher throughput per watt compared to leading CPU-based and accelerator-based read mappers, respectively, all without compromising accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 基因组测序因其在个性化医疗、疾病爆发追踪和进化研究等应用中的关键作用而成为计算生物学的中心焦点。基因组研究通常从测序开始，测序会产生数百万至数十亿的短 DNA 片段（称为读数）。从这些读取中提取有意义的生物学见解需要一个称为读取映射的计算密集型步骤，其中每个读取都与参考基因组进行比对。短读段的读段映射有两种形式：单端和双端，后者由于其更高的准确性和对高级分析的支持而更为普遍。由于计算密集型动态编程的广泛使用，读取映射仍然是基因组分析中的主要性能瓶颈。先前的努力已尝试通过使用过滤器来识别并可能丢弃计算成本昂贵的匹配并利用硬件加速器来加速计算来减轻这种成本。这些方法虽然部分有效，但也有局限性。特别是，现有的过滤器通常对于双端读取无效，因为它们独立评估每个读取并表现出相对较低的过滤率。在这项工作中，我们提出了 GenPairX，一种硬件算法协同设计的加速器，可以有效地最小化配对端读取映射的计算负载，同时提高内存密集型操作的吞吐量。 GenPairX 引入了：(1) 一种新颖的过滤算法，它联合考虑成对的读取以提高过滤效率，以及一种轻量级对齐算法来取代大多数计算量大的动态编程操作，以及 (2) 两种专门的硬件机制来支持所提出的算法。所提出的硬件通过在高带宽内存通道上编排内存访问来满足读取过滤过程的高内存带宽需求，并通过简单的矢量化逻辑异或运算符加速候选读取的对齐。我们的评估表明，与最先进的解决方案相比，GenPairX 的性能得到了显着提高，与领先的基于 CPU 和基于加速器的读取映射器相比，每瓦吞吐量分别提高了 1575 美元和 1.43 美元，而且完全不影响准确性。

items.title: ESTroM: Element-Flow Architecture for Processing Sparse Tractable Probabilistic Models.
items.abstract: Probabilistic Circuits (PCs) models are emerging popular tractable probabilistic models. Their internal connections are represented in the form of directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) with sum nodes and product nodes, ensuring their internal parameter efficiency and model expressiveness in terms of probabilistic inference. Despite these algorithmic advantages, executing PC still faces graph structure deployment issues. PyJuice on GPU with the block-sparse parallel computation methods causes a parallelism-sparsity gap, while DAG-style processing does not take advantage of the repetitive characteristics of PC internal nodes, resulting in low throughput. To address this challenge, this work proposes the ESTroM, an efficient architecture that provides novel graph-element (nodes/edges) parallelism with sparsity-aware compilation. Through analysis of the sum/product node computational requirements, ESTroM core uses compressed matrices for sum/product nodes DAG representations, edge-based dataflow for product node processing, and node-based dataflow for sum node processing. With intra-core rewind and intercore multicast optimizations, we develop a prototype ESTrom chip and a demonstrative system for a PC-based neural lossless compression application. Our ablation experiments show ESTrom offers a speed improvement of $2.11 \sim 3.79 \times$ compared to the state-of-the-art DAG processing unit (DPU)-v2 with the same computing resources. Under various typical PC structures, ESTrom achieves a speedup of $18.7 \times$ compared to DPU-v2 and $3.9 \times$ compared to NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU with PyJuice framework. In terms of neural lossless compression, ESTroM demonstrates a $1.39 \times$ improvement in compression ratio compared to the industrial-standard Z-standard (Zstd) algorithms with the highest compression level, while offering $16.3 \sim 65.2 \times$ improvement in compression speed compared to Zstd on Intel Xeon Gold 6230. In a nutshell, this work develops novel graph element parallelism and element-flow architecture theory with practical prototype chips and systems, revealing a new hardware-perspective path for the “scaling law” of emerging tractable probabilistic models.
items.abstract_zh: 概率电路 (PC) 模型是新兴的、流行的、易于处理的概率模型。它们的内部连接以带有和节点和乘积节点的有向无环图（DAG）的形式表示，保证了它们在概率推理方面的内部参数效率和模型表达力。尽管有这些算法优势，执行 PC 仍然面临图结构部署问题。 GPU上的PyJuice采用块稀疏并行计算方法，导致并行稀疏性差距，而DAG式处理没有利用PC内部节点的重复特性，导致吞吐量较低。为了应对这一挑战，这项工作提出了 ESTroM，这是一种高效的架构，通过稀疏感知编译提供新颖的图元素（节点/边）并行性。通过对和/积节点计算需求的分析，ESTroM 核心使用压缩矩阵进行和/积节点 DAG 表示，使用基于边的数据流进行乘积节点处理，使用基于节点的数据流进行和节点处理。通过核内倒带和核间多播优化，我们开发了一个原型 ESTrom 芯片和一个用于基于 PC 的神经无损压缩应用的演示系统。我们的消融实验表明，与具有相同计算资源的最先进的 DAG 处理单元 (DPU)-v2 相比，ESTrom 的速度提高了 $2.11 \sim 3.79 \times$。在各种典型的 PC 结构下，ESTrom 与 DPU-v2 相比实现了 18.7 美元的加速，与采用 PyJuice 框架的 NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU 相比实现了 3.9 美元的加速。在神经无损压缩方面，ESTroM 与最高压缩级别的工业标准 Z 标准（Zstd）算法相比，压缩比提高了 1.39 美元，同时与 Intel Xeon Gold 6230 上的 Zstd 相比，压缩速度提高了 16.3 美元/sim 65.2 倍。简而言之，这项工作通过实用的原型芯片和系统开发了新颖的图元素并行性和元素流架构理论，揭示了一种新的计算方法。新兴的易处理概率模型的“缩放定律”的硬件视角路径。

items.title: Intermittence-Aware Cache Compression.
items.abstract: Cache compressions are proven effective in improving the performance of caches in conventional processors. They compress data into a smaller size, allowing caches to accommodate more blocks. This helps reduce cache misses and expensive memory accesses, ultimately improving performance. However, conventional cache compression is less effective for energy harvesting systems (EHSs) which experience frequent power failure, as many compressed blocks end up not being used before their loss upon power outage. This wastes hard-won energy, which would otherwise be used for making more program progress. To address this issue, this paper introduces Kagura, an adaptive cache compression extension with frequent power failure in mind. Specifically, Kagura disables cache compression when it finds out that many cached blocks are unlikely to be reused before the next power outage. That way, Kagura avoids the energy waste on useless compressions/decompressions, and the resulting speedup is on par with the ideal intermittenceaware cache compressor. Experimental results show that when combined with an existing cache compressor, Kagura reduces the total energy consumption by an average of $\mathbf{4. 5 3 \%}$ (up to 16.21 %) and improves the performance by an average of 4.74 % (up to $\mathbf{1 7. 8 7 \%}$ ) compared to the baseline EHS without cache compression.
items.abstract_zh: 事实证明，缓存压缩可以有效提高传统处理器中缓存的性能。它们将数据压缩为更小的大小，从而允许缓存容纳更多的块。这有助于减少缓存未命中和昂贵的内存访问，最终提高性能。然而，传统的缓存压缩对于频繁断电的能量收集系统（EHS）来说效果较差，因为许多压缩块在断电时丢失之前最终没有被使用。这浪费了来之不易的能源，否则这些能源本可以用于取得更多的项目进展。为了解决这个问题，本文引入了 Kagura，这是一种考虑到频繁断电的自适应缓存压缩扩展。具体来说，当 Kagura 发现许多缓存块在下次断电之前不太可能被重用时，它会禁用缓存压缩。这样，Kagura 就可以避免无用的压缩/解压缩上的能源浪费，并且由此产生的加速效果与理想的间歇感知缓存压缩器相当。实验结果表明，与现有缓存压缩器结合使用时，Kagura 平均降低了总能耗 $\mathbf{4.与没有缓存压缩的基准 EHS 相比，性能提高了 5 3 \%}$ （高达 16.21 %），性能平均提高了 4.74 % （高达 $\mathbf{1 7. 8 7 \%}$ ）。

items.title: FractalCloud: A Fractal-Inspired Architecture for Efficient Large-Scale Point Cloud Processing.
items.abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) point clouds are increasingly used in applications such as autonomous driving, robotics, and virtual reality (VR). Point-based neural networks (PNNs) have demonstrated strong performance in point cloud analysis, originally targeting small-scale inputs. However, as PNNs evolve to process large-scale point clouds with hundreds of thousands of points, all-to-all computation and global memory access in point cloud processing introduce substantial overhead, causing $O\left(n^{2}\right)$ computational complexity and memory traffic where $n$ is the number of points. Existing accelerators, primarily optimized for small-scale workloads, overlook this challenge and scale poorly due to inefficient partitioning and non-parallel architectures. To address these issues, we propose FractalCloud, a fractal-inspired hardware architecture for efficient large-scale 3D point cloud processing. FractalCloud introduces two key optimizations: (1) a co-designed Fractal method for shape-aware and hardware-friendly partitioning, and (2) block-parallel point operations that decompose and parallelize all point operations. A dedicated hardware design with on-chip fractal and flexible parallelism further enables fully parallel processing within limited memory resources. Implemented in 28 nm technology as a chip layout with a core area of $1.5 ~\text{mm}^{2}$ , FractalCloud achieves $21.7 \times$ speedup and $27 \times$ energy reduction over state-of-the-art accelerators while maintaining network accuracy, demonstrating its scalability and efficiency for PNN inference. The code for FractalCloud is available at https://github.com/Yuzhe-Fu/FractalCloud.
items.abstract_zh: 三维 (3D) 点云越来越多地应用于自动驾驶、机器人和虚拟现实 (VR) 等应用中。基于点的神经网络（PNN）在点云分析中表现出了强大的性能，最初针对的是小规模输入。然而，随着 PNN 发展到处理具有数十万个点的大规模点云，点云处理中的所有计算和全局内存访问会带来大量开销，导致 $O\left(n^{2}\right)$ 计算复杂度和内存流量，其中 $n$ 是点的数量。现有的加速器主要针对小规模工作负载进行了优化，但忽视了这一挑战，并且由于分区效率低下和非并行架构而导致扩展能力较差。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 FractalCloud，这是一种受分形启发的硬件架构，用于高效的大规模 3D 点云处理。 FractalCloud 引入了两个关键优化：(1) 共同设计的分形方法，用于形状感知和硬件友好的分区，以及 (2) 块并行点操作，可分解和并行化所有点操作。具有片上分形和灵活并行性的专用硬件设计进一步实现了有限内存资源内的完全并行处理。 FractalCloud 采用 28 nm 技术作为芯片布局实现，核心面积为 $1.5 ~\text{mm}^{2}$ ，与最先进的加速器相比，FractalCloud 实现了 $21.7 \times$ 的加速和 $27 \times$ 的能耗，同时保持了网络精度，展示了其 PNN 推理的可扩展性和效率。 FractalCloud 的代码可在 https://github.com/Yuzhe-Fu/FractalCloud 获取。

items.title: Cambricon-CIM: Enabling Energy-Efficient and Error-Resilient Analog CIM Acceleration via Reformation of Coding Bases.
items.abstract: Recently, multi-bit slicing has emerged as a promising technique to improve the energy efficiency of charge-domain Compute-In-Memory (CIM) accelerators by reducing the number of Analog-to-Digital (A/D) conversions. However, multi-bit slicing requires shift-and-add operations to reconstruct outputs, which exponentially amplify errors and cause significant accuracy degradation. Existing works mainly rely on hardware-aware retraining or noise-suppression techniques, incurring considerable design or power overhead. Thus, multi-bit CIM designs often face the dilemma of trading off energy efficiency for error resilience. In this paper, we propose Cambricon-CIM, a charge-domain multi-bit CIM accelerator that achieves both high energy efficiency and strong error resilience, without requiring retraining. The core insight is that the error amplification is proportional to digit weights; and by redefining these digit weights with smaller non-binary coding bases, it is possible to reduce the total error amplification. Leveraging this principle, CambriconCIM dynamically selects the minimal coding bases for every analog dot-product. With novel circuit and architectural support, Cambricon-CIM enables fast, low-overhead reconfiguration of coding bases at runtime. Experimental results show that Cambricon-CIM achieves 2.27× energy efficiency and 3.06× performance over RAELLA, a state-of-the-art error-resilient multi-bit slicing CIM architecture.
items.abstract_zh: 最近，多位切片已成为一种有前景的技术，可通过减少模数 (A/D) 转换次数来提高电荷域内存计算 (CIM) 加速器的能效。然而，多位切片需要移位加法运算来重建输出，这会指数级放大误差并导致精度显着下降。现有的工作主要依赖于硬件感知的再训练或噪声抑制技术，会产生相当大的设计或功耗开销。因此，多位 CIM 设计经常面临在能源效率和错误恢复之间进行权衡的困境。在本文中，我们提出了 Cambricon-CIM，这是一种电荷域多位 CIM 加速器，无需重新训练即可实现高能效和强大的错误恢复能力。核心见解是误差放大与数字权重成正比；通过用更小的非二进制编码基重新定义这些数字权重，可以减少总的误差放大。利用这一原理，CambriconCIM 为每个模拟点积动态选择最小编码基础。凭借新颖的电路和架构支持，Cambricon-CIM 可在运行时快速、低开销地重新配置编码库。实验结果表明，与最先进的容错多位切片 CIM 架构 RAELLA 相比，Cambricon-CIM 的能效提高了 2.27 倍，性能提高了 3.06 倍。

items.title: DP-HLS: A High-Level Synthesis Framework for Accelerating Dynamic Programming Algorithms in Bioinformatics.
items.abstract: Dynamic programming (DP) is a widely used algorithmic paradigm, particularly in bioinformatics, finding applications in a wide spectrum of tasks, including read assembly, homology search, gene annotation, basecalling, and phylogenetic inference. Due to its computationally intensive nature, many ASIC- and FPGA-based accelerators have been proposed in recent years to accelerate specific tasks. However, DP algorithms in bioinformatics can vary considerably, and most existing solutions are customized for a single application, representing just one design point within the broader DP space. These implementations typically rely on low-level hardware description languages (HDLs), often requiring months of manual implementation effort. This paper introduces DP-HLS, a novel framework based on High-Level Synthesis (HLS) that simplifies and accelerates the development of a vast set of bioinformatically relevant 2-D DP algorithms in hardware. DP-HLS achieves this by introducing a new abstraction layer that decouples the front-end specification from predefined HLS-based back-end optimizations, enabling users to efficiently develop new 2-D DP kernels in C++ and deploy them on FPGAs without needing any expertise in hardware design or HLS. In our experience, DP-HLS significantly reduced the development time of new kernels (months to days) and produced designs with comparable resource utilization to open-source hand-coded HDL-based implementations and performance within $\mathbf{7. 7 - 1 6. 8 \%}$ margin. DP-HLS is compatible with AWS ® EC2 F1 FPGA instances. To showcase its versatility, we implemented 15 diverse 2-D DP kernels using the DP-HLS framework, achieving $1.38-41 \times$ improved cost-efficiency over state-of-the-art GPU and CPU baselines and providing the first open-source FPGA implementation for several of them. The DP-HLS codebase is available freely under the MIT license at https://github.com/TurakhiaLab/DP-HLS.
items.abstract_zh: 动态编程 (DP) 是一种广泛使用的算法范式，特别是在生物信息学中，在多种任务中都有应用，包括读组装、同源搜索、基因注释、碱基调用和系统发育推理。由于计算密集型的特性，近年来人们提出了许多基于 ASIC 和 FPGA 的加速器来加速特定任务。然而，生物信息学中的 DP 算法可能存在很大差异，并且大多数现有解决方案都是针对单个应用程序定制的，仅代表更广泛的 DP 空间中的一个设计点。这些实现通常依赖于低级硬件描述语言 (HDL)，通常需要数月的手动实现工作。本文介绍了 DP-HLS，这是一种基于高级综合 (HLS) 的新型框架，可简化并加速硬件中大量生物信息相关的 2-D DP 算法的开发。 DP-HLS 通过引入新的抽象层来实现这一目标，该抽象层将前端规范与预定义的基于 HLS 的后端优化解耦，使用户能够使用 C++ 高效开发新的 2-D DP 内核并将其部署在 FPGA 上，而无需任何硬件设计或 HLS 方面的专业知识。根据我们的经验，DP-HLS 显着缩短了新内核的开发时间（数月到数天），并在 $\mathbf{7. 7 - 1 6. 8 \%}$ 保证金。 DP-HLS 与 AWS ® EC2 F1 FPGA 实例兼容。为了展示其多功能性，我们使用 DP-HLS 框架实现了 15 个不同的 2-D DP 内核，与最先进的 GPU 和 CPU 基准相比，成本效率提高了 1.38-41 美元，并为其中的几个提供了第一个开源 FPGA 实现。 DP-HLS 代码库可根据 MIT 许可证免费获得，网址为 https://github.com/TurakhiaLab/DP-HLS。

items.title: HR-DCIM: High-Reliability Floating-Point Digital CIM Architecture With Unified Low-Cost Iterative Error Correction.
items.abstract: Digital computing-in-memory (CIM) is a promising computing paradigm for the neural network (NN) acceleration. However, during the actual deployment process of digital CIM chips, we find that existing digital CIM designs face severe computing reliability issues, which are crucial for real product development but remain underexplored. Therefore, this work pioneers a systematic computing reliability analysis for digital CIM across off-memory and in-memory levels. We find that both the off-memory floating-point (FP) exponent alignment and the in-memory random cell bit-flip errors impair digital CIM's computing reliability, causing significant truncation and bit-flip accuracy loss. Critically, existing reliability solutions are incompatible with the unique multi-row accumulation structure of digital CIM, which either severely damage digital CIM's performance or result in prohibitive overhead. To address above challenges, we propose HR-DCIM: a highreliability FP digital CIM architecture featuring unified lowcost iterative error correction. Specifically, for the off-memory reliability, we propose an exponent-mantissa joint-alignment mechanism to repurpose inherent invalid bits of aligned mantissas as compensation bits to reduce alignment truncation loss, without damaging digital CIM's performance. Then, for the in-memory reliability, we propose a remainder aliasing-based unified multiply-accumulation (MAC) error correction mechanism to correct possible MAC errors caused by various cell error cases with low-cost iteration. Experimental results show that the proposed techniques enable digital CIM to maintain high performance and efficiency across various operating voltage conditions without significant accuracy loss.
items.abstract_zh: 内存数字计算（CIM）是一种有前景的神经网络（NN）加速计算范例。然而，在数字CIM芯片的实际部署过程中，我们发现现有的数字CIM设计面临严重的计算可靠性问题，这对于实际产品开发至关重要，但仍未得到充分探索。因此，这项工作开创了跨内存和内存级别的数字 CIM 系统计算可靠性分析。我们发现内存外浮点 (FP) 指数对齐和内存中随机单元位翻转错误都会损害数字 CIM 的计算可靠性，导致严重的截断和位翻转精度损失。关键的是，现有的可靠性解决方案与数字 CIM 独特的多行累积结构不兼容，这要么严重损害数字 CIM 的性能，要么导致过高的开销。为了应对上述挑战，我们提出了 HR-DCIM：一种具有统一低成本迭代纠错功能的高可靠性 FP 数字 CIM 架构。具体来说，对于内存外可靠性，我们提出了一种指数尾数联合对齐机制，将对齐尾数的固有无效位重新用作补偿位，以减少对齐截断损失，而不损害数字CIM的性能。然后，对于内存中的可靠性，我们提出了一种基于余数别名的统一乘法累加（MAC）纠错机制，以通过低成本迭代来纠正由各种单元错误情况引起的可能的MAC错误。实验结果表明，所提出的技术使数字 CIM 能够在各种工作电压条件下保持高性能和效率，而不会造成显着的精度损失。

items.title: Peregrine: Accelerating TFHE Bootstrapping on GPUs via Multi-Level External Product Co-Design.
items.abstract: Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) is a ground-breaking cryptographic technology that enables computation directly on encrypted data, but its practical adoption continues to be hindered by high computational costs. GPUs have emerged as an increasingly attractive acceleration platform, offering massive parallelism and architectural flexibility to accommodate rapidly evolving FHE algorithms. Despite notable advances, most efforts remain confined to isolated, single-level optimizations, limiting their ability to push performance boundaries. In this paper, we present Peregrine, an efficient GPU-based TFHE acceleration built upon multi-level co-design of external product (EP) operations across parallelism, implementation, and scheduling. First, we propose a synchronization-free key unrolling technique that restructures the execution pipeline via operator decoupling, thereby unlocking greater EP-level parallelism. Second, we consolidate fragmented operators into a matrix-centric execution pattern, yielding a high-arithmetic-intensity kernel that substantially enhances external product efficiency. Third, we propose a hierarchical tiling strategy that reformats ring ciphertexts into the Module structure and schedules polynomiallevel tiles for fine-grained GPU mapping of EP operations. Experimental results show that Peregrine outperforms up to $176.9 \times$ and $2.2 \times$ over state-of-the-art CPU and GPU baselines, respectively, demonstrating its strong applicability to security-critical workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 全同态加密（FHE）是一种突破性的密码技术，可以直接对加密数据进行计算，但其实际采用仍然受到高计算成本的阻碍。 GPU 已成为越来越有吸引力的加速平台，提供大规模并行性和架构灵活性，以适应快速发展的 FHE 算法。尽管取得了显着的进步，但大多数努力仍然局限于孤立的单级优化，限制了它们突破性能极限的能力。在本文中，我们介绍了 Peregrine，一种基于 GPU 的高效 TFHE 加速，它建立在跨并行性、实现和调度的外部乘积 (EP) 操作的多级协同设计之上。首先，我们提出了一种免同步密钥展开技术，通过运算符解耦来重构执行管道，从而解锁更大的 EP 级并行性。其次，我们将碎片化的算子整合为以矩阵为中心的执行模式，产生一个高算术强度的内核，大大提高了外部产品效率。第三，我们提出了一种分层平铺策略，将环密文重新格式化为模块结构，并调度多项式平铺以实现 EP 操作的细粒度 GPU 映射。实验结果表明，Peregrine 的性能分别比最先进的 CPU 和 GPU 基准高出 176.9 美元和 2.2 美元，这证明了其对安全关键型工作负载的强大适用性。

items.title: LoCaLUT: Harnessing Capacity-Computation Tradeoffs for LUT-Based Inference in DRAM-PIM.
items.abstract: Lookup tables (LUTs) have recently gained attention as an alternative compute mechanism that maps input operands to precomputed results, eliminating the need for arithmetic logic. LUTs not only reduce logic complexity, but also naturally support diverse numerical precisions without requiring separate circuits for each bitwidth—an increasingly important feature in quantized DNNs. This creates a favorable tradeoff in PIM: memory capacity can be used in place of logic to increase computational throughput, aligning well with DRAM-PIM architectures that offer high bandwidth and easily available memory but limited logic density. In this work, we explore this capacity-computation tradeoff in LUT-based PIM designs, where memory capacity is traded for performance by packing multiple MAC operations into a single LUT lookup. Building on this insight, we propose LoCaLUT, a PIM-based design for efficient low-bit quantized DNN inference using operation-packed LUTs. First, we observe that these LUTs contain extensive redundancy and introduce LUT canonicalization, which eliminates duplicate entries to reduce LUT size. Second, we propose reordering LUT, a lightweight auxiliary LUT that remaps weight vectors to their canonical form required by LUT canonicalization with a simple LUT lookup. Third, we propose LUT slice streaming, a novel execution strategy that exploits the DRAM-buffer hierarchy by streaming only relevant LUT columns into the buffer and reusing them across multiple weight vectors. Evaluated on a real system based on UPMEM devices, we demonstrate a geometric mean speedup of $1.82 \times$ across various numeric precisions and DNN models. We believe LoCaLUT opens a path toward scalable, low-logic PIM designs tailored for LUT-based DNN inference. Our implementation of LoCaLUT is available at https://github.com/AIS-SNU/LoCaLUT.
items.abstract_zh: 查找表 (LUT) 作为一种替代计算机制最近引起了人们的关注，该机制将输入操作数映射到预先计算的结果，从而消除了对算术逻辑的需求。 LUT 不仅降低了逻辑复杂性，而且自然地支持不同的数值精度，而无需为每个位宽使用单独的电路——这是量化 DNN 中日益重要的功能。这在 PIM 中创造了有利的权衡：可以使用内存容量代替逻辑来提高计算吞吐量，与提供高带宽和易于使用的内存但逻辑密度有限的 DRAM-PIM 架构很好地结合起来。在这项工作中，我们探索了基于 LUT 的 PIM 设计中的容量与计算权衡，其中通过将多个 MAC 操作打包到单个 LUT 查找中来交换内存容量以换取性能。基于这一见解，我们提出了 LoCaLUT，这是一种基于 PIM 的设计，可使用运算填充 LUT 进行高效的低位量化 DNN 推理。首先，我们观察到这些 LUT 包含大量冗余并引入 LUT 规范化，从而消除重复条目以减小 LUT 大小。其次，我们提出重新排序 LUT，这是一种轻量级辅助 LUT，它通过简单的 LUT 查找将权重向量重新映射到 LUT 规范化所需的规范形式。第三，我们提出了 LUT 切片流式传输，这是一种新颖的执行策略，它通过仅将相关的 LUT 列流式传输到缓冲区并在多个权重向量中重用它们来利用 DRAM 缓冲区层次结构。在基于 UPMEM 设备的真实系统上进行评估，我们证明了各种数值精度和 DNN 模型的几何平均加速率为 1.82 倍。我们相信 LoCaLUT 为基于 LUT 的 DNN 推理量身定制的可扩展、低逻辑 PIM 设计开辟了一条道路。我们的 LoCaLUT 实现可在 https://github.com/AIS-SNU/LoCaLUT 上找到。

items.title: Swift: High-Performance Sparse-Dense Matrix Multiplication on GPUs.
items.abstract: Sparse-Dense Matrix Multiplication (SpMM) on GPUs has gained significant attention because of its importance in modern applications and the increasing computing power of GPUs in the last decade. Previous SpMM studies have focused on the importance of storage format and load balance for the overall performance of SpMM on GPUs. However, very little attention has been paid to the efficacy of coalesced memory access in improving the efficiency of data loading, which incurs a notable overhead that amounts to an average of more than 32% of the overall performance, according to our experimental observation. Existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) solutions fail to adequately support coalesced memory access of both sparse and dense matrices between the global memory and threads on GPUs. In this paper, we propose an efficient algorithm called Swift. 1 1 Swift is available at https://github.com/MinttHu/Swift.git that speeds up the loading of both sparse and dense matrices of SpMM on modern GPUs. Leveraging coalesced memory access, Swift achieves high loading efficiency by sorting both the columns of the sparse matrix and elements of the dense matrix based on the number of non-zero elements and balancing the load by handling the regular and irregular parts differently and judiciously. Swift takes the Compressed Sparse Column format as an implementation case study to prove the concept and gain insights. We conduct a comprehensive comparison of Swift with four SOTA solutions: ASpT, cuSPARSE, RoDe, and Sputnik, using the full SuiteSparse Matrix Collection as the workload. The experimental results on RTX 4080s, RTX 3090Ti, A100, and V100 demonstrate that our method outperforms the baselines significantly.
items.abstract_zh: GPU 上的稀疏密集矩阵乘法 (SpMM) 因其在现代应用中的重要性以及过去十年中 GPU 计算能力的不断增强而受到广泛关注。之前的 SpMM 研究重点关注存储格式和负载平衡对于 GPU 上 SpMM 整体性能的重要性。然而，很少有人关注合并内存访问在提高数据加载效率方面的功效，根据我们的实验观察，这会产生显着的开销，平均占整体性能的 32% 以上。现有的最先进 (SOTA) 解决方案无法充分支持全局内存和 GPU 线程之间稀疏矩阵和稠密矩阵的合并内存访问。在本文中，我们提出了一种称为 Swift 的高效算法。 1 1 Swift 可在 https://github.com/MinttHu/Swift.git 上获取，它可以加快现代 GPU 上 SpMM 稀疏矩阵和稠密矩阵的加载速度。利用合并内存访问，Swift 通过根据非零元素的数量对稀疏矩阵的列和稠密矩阵的元素进行排序，并通过不同且明智地处理规则和不规则部分来平衡负载，从而实现高加载效率。 Swift 以压缩稀疏列格式作为实施案例研究来证明这一概念并获得见解。我们使用完整的 SuiteSparse Matrix Collection 作为工作负载，对 Swift 与四种 SOTA 解决方案进行了全面比较：ASpT、cuSPARSE、RoDe 和 Sputnik。 RTX 4080s、RTX 3090Ti、A100 和 V100 上的实验结果表明，我们的方法显着优于基线。

items.title: µShare: Non-Intrusive Kernel Co-Locating on NVIDIA GPUs.
items.abstract: The hardware scheduler on NVIDIA GPUs is highly inefficient in utilizing micro-architectural hardware resources. It places blocks from the same kernel within the same GPU Streaming Multiprocessor (SM) core, resulting in a stacking colocating problem, where identical blocks are placed within the same SM core, saturating only a subset of intra-SM hardware resources while leaving others underutilized. The primary challenge in addressing this issue is that the NVIDIA hardware is closed-source, preventing us from directly modifying the hardware scheduler. To bridge the semantic gap between the resource demands of kernels and the scheduler, we introduce µ Share, which enables intra-SM scattered colocating of kernels through a non-intrusive half-plus blocksize shaping method. It shapes the blocksize of kernels to a halfplus blocksize (i.e., slightly more than half of the SM's thread capacity), scattering identical blocks of the same kernel across different SMs. It further adopts a time-shifted launching method to reduce intra-SM resource contention. Compared to state-of-the-art systems, µ Share does not require intrusive modifications to hardware or kernel code, yet it can still improve inference throughput by 26.90%-54.09% and increases low-level hardware utilization by 38.53%-61.15%.
items.abstract_zh: NVIDIA GPU 上的硬件调度程序在利用微架构硬件资源方面效率非常低。它将来自同一内核的块放置在同一 GPU 流多处理器 (SM) 核心中，从而导致堆栈共置问题，即相同的块放置在同一 SM 核心中，仅使 SM 内硬件资源的子集饱和，而其他资源未得到充分利用。解决这个问题的主要挑战是NVIDIA硬件是闭源的，这使得我们无法直接修改硬件调度程序。为了弥合内核和调度程序的资源需求之间的语义差距，我们引入了 µ Share，它通过非侵入式半加块大小整形方法实现 SM 内分散的内核共置。它将内核的块大小调整为一半的块大小（即略大于 SM 线程容量的一半），将同一内核的相同块分散到不同的 SM 上。它还采用时移启动方法来减少SM内的资源争用。与最先进的系统相比，μ Share不需要对硬件或内核代码进行侵入性修改，但它仍然可以将推理吞吐量提高26.90%-54.09%，并将底层硬件利用率提高38.53%-61.15%。

items.title: FlashFuser: Expanding the Scale of Kernel Fusion for Compute-Intensive Operators via Inter-Core Connection.
items.abstract: The scaling of computation throughput continues to outpace improvements in memory bandwidth, making many deep learning workloads memory-bound. Kernel fusion is a key technique to alleviate this problem, but the fusion strategies of existing compilers and frameworks are limited to using local scratchpad memory. When the intermediate results exceed the limited capacity (such as FFN), the fusion fails. Although modern GPUs (like the NVIDIA H100) now incorporate an inter-core connection mechanism known as Distributed Shared Memory (DSM)—providing a larger, high-bandwidth, and low-latency on-chip memory pool—this hardware potential has yet to be exploited by software frameworks. To bridge this gap, we present FlashFuser, the first compiler framework to utilize inter-core connection for kernel fusion on modern GPUs. FlashFuser extends established fusion techniques to the DSM domain through three core contributions. First, we propose a powerful DSM-based communication abstraction that formalizes complex cluster-based data exchange patterns, such as reduce, shuffle and multiply. Second, we introduce a dataflow analyzer that generalizes loop scheduling, resource mapping, and tile selection to the distributed memory hierarchy; it determines the optimal execution order and tile sizes by quantifying data movement across memory levels. Finally, FlashFuser integrates these components into a unified search engine that employs analytical cost modeling and DSM-aware pruning strategies to efficiently discover the optimal execution plan. Our evaluation on an NVIDIA H100 GPU shows that FlashFuser reduces memory access by 58 % and delivers kernel speedups of $3.3 x$ against highly-tuned libraries and 4.1x against state-of-the-art compilers, resulting in a $1.24 \times$ end-to-end speedup.
items.abstract_zh: 计算吞吐量的扩展继续超过内存带宽的改进，使得许多深度学习工作负载受到内存限制。内核融合是缓解这一问题的关键技术，但现有编译器和框架的融合策略仅限于使用本地暂存器内存。当中间结果超过限制容量（如FFN）时，融合失败。尽管现代 GPU（如 NVIDIA H100）现在采用了一种称为分布式共享内存 (DSM) 的内核间连接机制，可提供更大、高带宽和低延迟的片上内存池，但这种硬件潜力尚未被软件框架充分利用。为了弥补这一差距，我们推出了 FlashFuser，这是第一个在现代 GPU 上利用内核间连接进行内核融合的编译器框架。 FlashFuser 通过三个核心贡献将已建立的融合技术扩展到 DSM 领域。首先，我们提出了一个强大的基于 DSM 的通信抽象，它形式化了复杂的基于集群的数据交换模式，例如减少、洗牌和乘法。其次，我们引入了一个数据流分析器，它将循环调度、资源映射和瓦片选择推广到分布式内存层次结构；它通过量化跨内存级别的数据移动来确定最佳执行顺序和切片大小。最后，FlashFuser 将这些组件集成到统一的搜索引擎中，该搜索引擎采用分析成本建模和 DSM 感知修剪策略来有效地发现最佳执行计划。我们对 NVIDIA H100 GPU 的评估表明，FlashFuser 减少了 58% 的内存访问，与高度调优的库相比，内核加速为 3.3 倍，与最先进的编译器相比，内核加速为 4.1 倍，从而实现了 1.24 美元×$ 的端到端加速。

items.title: SPLATONIC: Architectural Support for 3D Gaussian Splatting SLAM via Sparse Processing.
items.abstract: 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a promising direction for SLAM due to its high-fidelity reconstruction and rapid convergence. However, 3DGS-SLAM algorithms remain impractical for mobile platforms due to their high computational cost, especially for their tracking process. This work introduces Splatonic, a sparse and efficient realtime 3DGS-SLAM algorithm-hardware co-design for resourceconstrained devices. Inspired by classical SLAMs, we propose an adaptive sparse pixel sampling algorithm that reduces the number of rendered pixels by up to $256 \times$ while retaining accuracy. To unlock this performance potential on mobile GPUs, we design a novel pixel-based rendering pipeline that improves hardware utilization via Gaussian-parallel rendering and preemptive $\alpha$ -checking. Together, these optimizations yield up to $121.7 \times$ speedup on the bottleneck stages and $14.6 \times$ end-toend speedup on off-the-shelf GPUs. To further address new bottlenecks introduced by our rendering pipeline, we propose a pipelined architecture that simplifies the overall design while addressing newly emerged bottlenecks in projection and aggregation. Evaluated across four 3DGS-SLAM algorithms, Splatonic achieves up to $274.9 \times$ speedup and $4738.5 \times$ energy savings over mobile GPUs and up to $25.2 \times$ speedup and $241.1 \times$ energy savings over state-of-the-art accelerators, all with comparable accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 3D 高斯分布（3DGS）由于其高保真重建和快速收敛而成为 SLAM 的一个有前途的方向。然而，由于计算成本较高，尤其是跟踪过程，3DGS-SLAM 算法对于移动平台仍然不切实际。这项工作介绍了 Splatonic，这是一种针对资源受限设备的稀疏且高效的实时 3DGS-SLAM 算法-硬件协同设计。受经典 SLAM 的启发，我们提出了一种自适应稀疏像素采样算法，可在保持准确性的同时将渲染像素数量减少高达 256 美元\times$。为了释放移动 GPU 上的性能潜力，我们设计了一种新颖的基于像素的渲染管道，通过高斯并行渲染和抢占式 $\alpha$ 检查来提高硬件利用率。总之，这些优化在瓶颈阶段带来高达 121.7 美元的加速，在现成 GPU 上带来高达 14.6 美元的端到端加速。为了进一步解决渲染管道引入的新瓶颈，我们提出了一种管道架构，可以简化整体设计，同时解决投影和聚合中新出现的瓶颈。通过四种 3DGS-SLAM 算法进行评估，Splatonic 与移动 GPU 相比，可实现高达 274.9 美元的加速和 4738.5 美元的节能，与最先进的加速器相比，可实现高达 25.2 美元的加速和 241.1 美元的节能，所有这些都具有相当的精度。

items.title: GustavSNN: Unleashing the Power of Gustavson's Algorithm on SNN Acceleration with Column-Parallel Tick-Batch Dataflow.
items.abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) require sequential computation over long timesteps, introducing substantial memory and energy overheads due to frequent updates of neuron membrane potentials. Previous SNN accelerators address this by employing tick-batch techniques, which process all timesteps within a layer before moving on to the next. However, existing approaches rely on neuron-centric scheduling, limiting their ability to exploit temporal sparsity. In this work, we propose a novel scheduling approach along with its hardware architecture for Gustavson product (GP)-based SNN acceleration. We introduce a column-parallel tick-batch (CPTB) dataflow that partitions the spike matrix into multiple submatrices and processes each submatrix of a timestep in parallel while maintaining tickbatch semantics. To support this, we present the first GP-based SNN accelerator, named GustavSNN, which avoids accessing the global membrane potential memory by updating neuron states directly in local registers. In addition, we propose a non-zero row vector (NRV) spike format that enables fine-grained skipping of inactive spike rows. As a result, our proposed architecture achieves up to 11.8× higher energy efficiency (GOPS/W) than naïve GP-based accelerator and 1.43× higher energy efficiency compared to state-of-the-art SNN accelerators.
items.abstract_zh: 尖峰神经网络（SNN）需要长时间步长的顺序计算，由于神经元膜电位的频繁更新而引入大量的内存和能量开销。以前的 SNN 加速器通过采用滴答批处理技术来解决这个问题，该技术在进入下一层之前处理一层内的所有时间步。然而，现有的方法依赖于以神经元为中心的调度，限制了它们利用时间稀疏性的能力。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的调度方法及其硬件架构，用于基于 Gustavson 产品（GP）的 SNN 加速。我们引入了列并行滴答批（CPTB）数据流，它将尖峰矩阵划分为多个子矩阵，并并行处理时间步的每个子矩阵，同时保持滴答批语义。为了支持这一点，我们提出了第一个基于 GP 的 SNN 加速器，名为 GustavSNN，它通过直接更新本地寄存器中的神经元状态来避免访问全局膜电位存储器。此外，我们提出了一种非零行向量（NRV）尖峰格式，可以细粒度地跳过不活动的尖峰行。因此，我们提出的架构比基于 GP 的简单加速器的能效 (GOPS/W) 高出 11.8 倍，比最先进的 SNN 加速器高出 1.43 倍。

items.title: Toward Scalable Gate-Level Parallelism on Trapped-Ion Processors with Racetrack Electrodes.
items.abstract: A recent advancement in quantum computing shows a quantum advantage of certified randomness on the racetrack processor. This work investigates the execution efficiency of this architecture for general-purpose programs. We first explore the impact of increasing zones on runtime efficiency. Counterintuitively, our evaluations using variational programs reveal that expanding zones may degrade runtime performance under the existing scheduling policy. This degradation may be attributed to the increase in track length, which increases ion circulation overhead, offsetting the benefits of enhanced parallelism. To mitigate this, the proposed Plutarch exploits 3 strategies: (i) unitary decomposition and translation to maximize zone utilization, (ii) prioritizing the execution of nearby gates over ion circulation, and (iii) implementing shortcuts to provide the alternative path.
items.abstract_zh: 量子计算的最新进展显示了赛道处理器上经过认证的随机性的量子优势。这项工作研究了该架构对于通用程序的执行效率。我们首先探讨增加区域对运行时效率的影响。与直觉相反，我们使用变分程序的评估表明，在现有调度策略下，扩展区域可能会降低运行时性能。这种退化可能归因于磁道长度的增加，这增加了离子循环开销，抵消了增强并行性的好处。为了缓解这一问题，所提出的 Plutarch 采用了 3 种策略：(i) 单一分解和转换以最大化区域利用率，(ii) 优先执行附近的门而不是离子循环，以及 (iii) 实施捷径以提供替代路径。

items.title: Exploration of LLM Workload Reliability Based on di/dt Effects and Voltage Droops.
items.abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference workloads have emerged as a critical reliability challenge for cloud GPU systems. Unlike traditional workloads, the highly structured execution of LLMs creates large power oscillations. These oscillations become a vulnerability when their frequency aligns with the resonant modes of a GPU's power delivery network (PDN), leading to excessive voltage droops and unreliable operation. In this work, we present the first comprehensive profiling of LLM-induced power oscillations, revealing that many workloads generate oscillatory patterns in the MHz range-critically aligning with typical GPU PDN resonant frequencies and leading to excessive voltage droops. To systematically investigate this phenomenon, we developed a novel stressmark framework that generates workloads with controllable, high-frequency power oscillations and voltage droops. Our evaluation shows that operating at a resonant frequency induces voltage droops up to $2 \times$ larger than conventional workloads, exceeding critical noise margins. Critically, we find that real LLM workloads operating even near these frequencies generate significant voltage droops greater than 100 mV. Based on these findings, we propose a kernel staggering technique that mitigates this threat by shifting power oscillation frequencies away from resonance frequency, successfully reducing voltage droops and reducing reliability concerns. This work provides the first systematic understanding of LLM-PDN resonance and offers a practical solution to improve GPU reliability in AI cloud environments.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 推理工作负载已成为云 GPU 系统的关键可靠性挑战。与传统工作负载不同，LLM 的高度结构化执行会产生较大的功率振荡。当这些振荡的频率与 GPU 供电网络 (PDN) 的谐振模式一致时，这些振荡就会成为一个漏洞，从而导致电压过度下降和运行不可靠。在这项工作中，我们首次对 LLM 引起的功率振荡进行了全面分析，揭示了许多工作负载会生成 MHz 范围内的振荡模式，与典型的 GPU PDN 谐振频率严重一致，并导致电压过度下降。为了系统地研究这种现象，我们开发了一种新颖的压力标记框架，该框架可生成具有可控高频功率振荡和电压下降的工作负载。我们的评估表明，在谐振频率下运行会导致比传统工作负载大 2 美元\倍$的电压降，超过临界噪声容限。至关重要的是，我们发现即使在这些频率附近运行的实际 LLM 工作负载也会产生大于 100 mV 的显着压降。基于这些发现，我们提出了一种内核交错技术，该技术通过将功率振荡频率移离谐振频率来减轻这种威胁，成功地减少电压降并减少可靠性问题。这项工作首次系统地了解了LLM-PDN共振，并为提高AI云环境中GPU的可靠性提供了实用的解决方案。

items.title: Secret Caching Sauce for High-Performance Secure Memory.
items.abstract: In modern secure memory systems, confidentiality and integrity protection add additional metadata (called security metadata) that are accessed during memory data accesses. Security metadata are cached in on-chip metadata caches to reduce the access cost. However, prior studies overlook the differences between security metadata caching and data caching. Metadata caches are managed similarly to data caches. We identify three special features of security metadata caching. Due to these features, existing management methods face two problems. First, there are frequent security metadata accesses and many metadata cache misses, because the best metric (i.e., reuse distance) used to select replacement victims in data caches is suboptimal for metadata caches. Second, the miss penalty is large since misses suffer from long security metadata update and verification chains. The two problems significantly increase data access latency, thereby degrading system performance. We propose SECRET, a security metadata oriented cache management framework. SECRET reduces accesses and misses by selecting victims based on a new metric superior to reuse distances, called the connections between cached security metadata. We identify this metric by developing an optimal replacement decision search approach and analyzing the optimal decisions. SECRET reduces the miss penalty by actively breaking update chains and parallelizing verification chains. Experimental results show that SECRET reduces the data read (write) latency by $30.2 \%(59.9 \%)$ and improves system performance by 26.2 % compared with the state-of-the-art designs.
items.abstract_zh: 在现代安全内存系统中，机密性和完整性保护添加了在内存数据访问期间访问的附加元数据（称为安全元数据）。安全元数据缓存在片上元数据缓存中，以降低访问成本。然而，先前的研究忽略了安全元数据缓存和数据缓存之间的差异。元数据缓存的管理方式与数据缓存类似。我们确定了安全元数据缓存的三个特殊功能。由于这些特点，现有的管理方法面临两个问题。首先，存在频繁的安全元数据访问和许多元数据缓存未命中，因为用于在数据缓存中选择替换受害者的最佳指标（即重用距离）对于元数据缓存来说并不是最佳的。其次，由于未命中受到较长的安全元数据更新和验证链的影响，因此未命中的惩罚很大。这两个问题显着增加了数据访问延迟，从而降低了系统性能。我们提出了SECRET，一个面向安全元数据的缓存管理框架。 SECRET 通过基于优于重用距离（称为缓存安全元数据之间的连接）的新指标来选择受害者，从而减少访问和丢失。我们通过开发最佳替换决策搜索方法并分析最佳决策来确定该指标。 SECRET 通过主动打破更新链和并行化验证链来减少失误损失。实验结果表明，与最先进的设计相比，SECRET 将数据读（写）延迟降低了 $30.2 \%(59.9 \%)$，并将系统性能提高了 26.2 %。

items.title: AutoGNN: End-to-End Hardware-Driven Graph Preprocessing for Enhanced GNN Performance.
items.abstract: Graph neural network (GNN) inference faces significant bottlenecks in preprocessing, which often dominate overall inference latency. We introduce AutoGNN, an FPGA-based accelerator designed to address these challenges by leveraging FPGA's reconfigurability and specialized components. AutoGNN adapts to diverse graph inputs, efficiently performing computationally intensive tasks such as graph conversion and sampling. By utilizing components like adder trees, AutoGNN executes reduction operations in constant time, overcoming the limitations of serialization and synchronization on GPUs. AutoGNN integrates unified processing elements (UPEs) and single-cycle reducers (SCRs) to streamline GNN preprocessing. UPEs enable scalable parallel processing for edge sorting and unique vertex selection, while SCRs efficiently handle sequential tasks such as pointer array construction and subgraph reindexing. A user-level software framework dynamically profiles graph inputs, determines optimal configurations, and reprograms AutoGNN to handle varying workloads. Implemented on a $7 n \mathrm{m}$ enterprise FPGA, AutoGNN achieves up to $9.0 \times$ and $2.1 \times$ speedup compared to conventional and GPU-accelerated preprocessing systems, respectively, enabling high-performance GNN preprocessing across diverse datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 图神经网络 (GNN) 推理在预处理方面面临重大瓶颈，这通常主导整体推理延迟。我们推出 AutoGNN，这是一种基于 FPGA 的加速器，旨在通过利用 FPGA 的可重新配置性和专用组件来应对这些挑战。 AutoGNN 适应不同的图输入，有效地执行计算密集型任务，例如图转换和采样。通过利用加法器树等组件，AutoGNN 在恒定时间内执行归约运算，克服了 GPU 上序列化和同步的限制。 AutoGNN 集成了统一处理元件 (UPE) 和单周期减速器 (SCR) 以简化 GNN 预处理。 UPE 支持边缘排序和独特顶点选择的可扩展并行处理，而 SCR 则有效处理顺序任务，例如指针数组构造和子图重新索引。用户级软件框架动态分析图形输入、确定最佳配置并对 AutoGNN 重新编程以处理不同的工作负载。 AutoGNN 在 7 n \mathrm{m}$ 企业级 FPGA 上实现，与传统预处理系统和 GPU 加速预处理系统相比，分别实现了高达 9.0 美元和 2.1 美元的加速，从而实现了跨不同数据集的高性能 GNN 预处理。

items.title: Cyclone: Designing Efficient and Highly Parallel QCCD Architectural Codesigns for Fault Tolerant Quantum Memory.
items.abstract: Modular trapped-ion quantum computing hardware, known as Quantum Charge Coupled Devices (QCCDs) require shuttling operations in order to maintain effective all-to-all connectivity. Each module or trap can perform only one operation at a time, resulting in low intra-trap parallelism, but there is no restriction on operations happening on independent traps, enabling high inter-trap parallelism. Unlike their superconducting counterparts, the design space for QCCDs is relatively flexible and can be explored beyond the constraints of two-dimensional grids. In this work, we are motivated by the opportunity to explore the QCCD design space in the context of optimizing for non-topological CSS codes. In particular, current grid-based architectures significantly limit the performance of many promising, high-rate codes such as hypergraph product codes and bivariate bicycle codes. Many of these codes are highly parallelizable, meaning that with appropriate hardware layouts and matching software schedules, execution latency can be greatly reduced. Faster execution, in turn, reduces error accumulation from decoherence and heating, ultimately improving code performance when mapped to realistic hardware. However, current 2D grid designs suffer from numerous trap to trap “roadblocks”, forcing serialization and destroying the inherent parallelism of these codes. To address this, we propose Cyclone, a circular software-hardware codesign that departs from traditional 2D grids in favor of a flexible ring topology, where ancilla qubits move in lockstep. Cyclone eliminates roadblocks, bounds total movement, and enables high levels of parallelism, resulting in up to $4 \times$ speedup in execution times. In addition to temporal efficiency, Cyclone also offers large spatial efficiency when compared to a grid codesign. It requires fewer traps, fewer junctions, and only a constant number of Digital-toAnalog Converters (DAC), as opposed to grid architectures, where DAC count scales linearly with the number of traps. With hypergraph product codes, Cyclone achieves up to a $2 \times$ order of magnitude improvement in logical error rate, and with bivariate bicycle codes, this improvement reaches up to a $3 \times$ in order of magnitude. Spatially, Cyclone reduces the number of required traps and ancilla qubits by $2 \times$ . The overall spacetime improvement over a standard grid is up to $\sim 20 \times$ , demonstrating Cyclone as a scalable and efficient alternative to conventional 2D QCCD architectures.
items.abstract_zh: 模块化俘获离子量子计算硬件，称为量子电荷耦合器件 (QCCD)，需要穿梭操作才能保持有效的全面连接。每个模块或陷阱一次只能执行一项操作，导致陷阱内并行度较低，但对独立陷阱上发生的操作没有限制，从而实现陷阱间较高的并行度。与超导同类产品不同，QCCD 的设计空间相对灵活，可以超越二维网格的限制进行探索。在这项工作中，我们受到在优化非拓扑 CSS 代码的背景下探索 QCCD 设计空间的机会的激励。特别是，当前基于网格的架构极大地限制了许多有前途的高速率代码（例如超图产品代码和双变量自行车代码）的性能。其中许多代码是高度可并行的，这意味着通过适当的硬件布局和匹配的软件调度，可以大大减少执行延迟。更快的执行反过来又减少了退相干和加热造成的错误累积，最终提高了映射到实际硬件时的代码性能。然而，当前的 2D 网格设计面临着无数陷阱与陷阱的“障碍”，迫使序列化并破坏了这些代码固有的并行性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Cyclone，这是一种循环软件硬件协同设计，它脱离了传统的 2D 网格，转而采用灵活的环形拓扑，其中辅助量子位同步移动。 Cyclone 消除了障碍，限制了总体移动，并实现了高水平的并行性，从而使执行时间加快了 4 美元\倍$。除了时间效率之外，与网格协同设计相比，Cyclone 还提供了很高的空间效率。它需要更少的陷阱、更少的结点，并且只需要恒定数量的数模转换器 (DAC)，这与 DAC 数量随陷阱数量线性扩展的网格架构不同。使用超图产品代码，Cyclone 在逻辑错误率方面实现了高达 $2 \times$ 数量级的改进，而对于二元自行车代码，这种改进达到了 $3 \times$ 数量级。在空间上，Cyclone 将所需的陷阱和辅助量子比特的数量减少了 $2 \times$ 。相对于标准网格的整体时空改进高达 $\sim 20 \times$ ，证明 Cyclone 是传统 2D QCCD 架构的可扩展且高效的替代方案。

items.title: GyRot: Leveraging Hidden Synergy Between Rotation and Fine-Grained Group Quantization for Low-Bit LLM Inference.
items.abstract: Low-bit quantization is essential for efficient LLM inference, and both rotation and fine-grained group quantization have shown individual promise. However, their combination often leads to accuracy degradation or hardware overhead due to a mismatch between the global nature of rotation and the localized behavior of group scaling. We propose GyRot, a quantization framework and hardware accelerator that bridges this gap through algorithm-hardware co-design. GyRot introduces Coarse Rotation, Fine Grouping (CoRFiG) and Harmonic-Aligned Permutation (HAP) to enable cooperative integration of rotation and group quantization, enhancing quantizability while relaxing scaling factor precision. To further reduce hardware cost, we reformulate asymmetric quantization and introduce a zero-point rounding strategy that enables fully integer dequantization. Implemented on an INT4-based tensor PE architecture, GyRot achieves state-of-the-art 4-bit accuracy across LLaMA-family models, while delivering up to 3.4× speedup and 3.6× energy efficiency over baseline LLM accelerators. These results validate GyRot's practical effectiveness for scalable and energy-efficient LLM deployment.
items.abstract_zh: 低位量化对于高效的 LLM 推理至关重要，旋转和细粒度组量化都显示出了各自的前景。然而，由于旋转的全局性质和组缩放的局部行为之间的不匹配，它们的组合通常会导致精度下降或硬件开销。我们提出了 GyRot，一种量化框架和硬件加速器，通过算法-硬件协同设计弥补了这一差距。 GyRot 引入了粗旋转、精细分组 (CoRFiG) 和谐波对齐排列 (HAP)，以实现旋转和组量化的协作集成，增强量化能力，同时放宽缩放因子精度。为了进一步降低硬件成本，我们重新制定了非对称量化并引入了零点舍入策略，以实现完全整数反量化。 GyRot 在基于 INT4 的张量 PE 架构上实现，在 LLaMA 系列模型中实现了最先进的 4 位精度，同时比基准 LLM 加速器提供高达 3.4 倍的加速和 3.6 倍的能源效率。这些结果验证了 GyRot 对于可扩展且节能的 LLM 部署的实际有效性。

items.title: IVE: An Accelerator for Single-Server Private Information Retrieval Using Versatile Processing Elements.
items.abstract: Private information retrieval (PIR) is an essential cryptographic protocol for privacy-preserving applications, enabling a client to retrieve a record from a server's database without revealing which record was requested. Single-server PIR based on homomorphic encryption has particularly gained immense attention for its ease of deployment and reduced trust assumptions. However, single-server PIR remains impractical due to its high computational and memory bandwidth demands. Specifically, reading the entirety of large databases from storage, such as SSDs, severely limits its performance. To address this, we propose IVE, an accelerator for single-server PIR with a systematic extension that enables practical retrieval from large databases using DRAM. Recent advances in DRAM capacity allow PIR for large databases to be served entirely from DRAM, removing its dependence on storage bandwidth.
